meta.name: EU Transportation Rules — Document QA Test Set
meta.description: 30 questions over the 10-document EU transportation corpus. Each question is answerable from a single source document. reference_answer + key_facts define ground truth; the LLM judge uses them to score candidate answers.
meta.grading: llm-judge (key_facts are required-fact anchors for the judge; verdict in {correct, partial, incorrect} -> score in {1.0, 0.5, 0.0})

questions.question: For a category A1 motorcycle licence, what is the maximum engine capacity and maximum power output?
questions.reference_answer: Engine capacity up to 125 cc and power up to 11 kW (power-to-weight ratio no greater than 0.1 kW/kg).

questions.question: What is the maximum number of passenger seats (excluding the driver) and the maximum vehicle length permitted under category D1?
questions.reference_answer: More than 8 but no more than 16 passenger seats, and a maximum length of 8 metres.

questions.question: Under what condition may a member state issue a category B licence with an administrative validity of up to 15 years instead of 10?
questions.reference_answer: When the licence card is issued before the holder turns 65.

questions.question: What is the maximum legal motorway speed limit in Poland?
questions.reference_answer: 140 km/h on autostradas — the highest legal motorway limit in the EU.

questions.question: On a French motorway, to what speed is the limit reduced in the rain, and to what speed when visibility drops below 50 metres?
questions.reference_answer: From 130 km/h down to 110 km/h in the rain, and down to 50 km/h when visibility is below 50 metres.

questions.question: On what date did France reduce its default two-lane rural road limit from 90 km/h to 80 km/h?
questions.reference_answer: 1 July 2018.

questions.question: What is the general blood alcohol concentration (BAC) limit for drivers in Malta?
questions.reference_answer: 0.8 g/L — the highest general limit in the bloc.

questions.question: Which EU member states enforce an absolute zero-tolerance BAC limit of 0.0 g/L for all drivers (not just novices)?
questions.reference_answer: Czechia, Hungary, Romania, and Slovakia.

questions.question: In Germany, at what BAC does drink-driving become a criminal offence of absolute unfitness to drive, and what is the maximum prison term for a first offence at that level?
questions.reference_answer: At a BAC of 1.1 g/L or above (Fahruntüchtigkeit, §316 StGB); the maximum prison term for a first offence is 1 year.

questions.question: Under EU Directive 2014/45/EU, when is a new private car's first periodic technical inspection due, and how often afterwards (the '4-2-2 rule')?
questions.reference_answer: First inspection 4 years after initial registration, then every 2 years.

questions.question: What is the EU-wide minimum legal tyre tread depth for passenger car tyres?
questions.reference_answer: 1.6 mm, measured across the central three-quarters of the tread width around the full circumference.

questions.question: What NOx emission limit applies to a Euro 6d diesel passenger car, and what NOx Conformity Factor did Euro 6d introduce for Real Driving Emissions testing?
questions.reference_answer: 80 mg/km NOx limit, with a Conformity Factor of 1.43 (reduced from 2.1 under Euro 6d-TEMP).

questions.question: Under the Vienna Convention, what shape is the 'Give Way' sign?
questions.reference_answer: An inverted (downward-pointing) equilateral triangle with a red border and white interior.

questions.question: In the Vienna Convention signal priority hierarchy, which authority ranks highest: police signals, traffic lights, road signs, or road markings?
questions.reference_answer: Police officer (or authorised traffic controller) signals rank highest.

questions.question: What does the simultaneous illumination of red and amber traffic lights together mean?
questions.reference_answer: Prepare to move — the signal is about to change to green; do not proceed yet.

questions.question: Which two EU member states drive on the left-hand side of the road?
questions.reference_answer: Malta and Cyprus.

questions.question: On what date did the UK change its international vehicle country code from GB to UK?
questions.reference_answer: 28 September 2021.

questions.question: How much does the Swiss motorway vignette cost and how long is it valid?
questions.reference_answer: CHF 40, annual-only, valid from 1 December of the prior year until 31 January the following year (about 14 months).

questions.question: What colour is the German Umweltplakette badge required by all currently active German Umweltzonen?
questions.reference_answer: Green.

questions.question: What Crit'Air category is assigned to a Euro 6 diesel car in France?
questions.reference_answer: Crit'Air 2 (yellow).

questions.question: From 2025 onwards, what minimum Euro standard must a diesel passenger car meet to enter the Brussels Low Emission Zone?
questions.reference_answer: Euro 6.

questions.question: Under EU rules, until what height or age must a child use an appropriate child restraint system?
questions.reference_answer: Until they reach 135 cm in height OR 12 years of age, whichever comes first.

questions.question: On a motorway, what is the minimum distance behind a stationary vehicle at which the warning triangle must be placed?
questions.reference_answer: At least 100 metres behind the vehicle.

questions.question: From what date must new motorcycle helmet models submitted for EU type approval meet the ECE 22.06 standard rather than ECE 22.05?
questions.reference_answer: From January 2024 (existing ECE 22.05 helmets already on the market remain legal).

questions.question: How many hours of periodic training must a professional HGV driver complete every 5 years to maintain their Driver CPC?
questions.reference_answer: 35 hours.

questions.question: Under Regulation (EC) 561/2006, what is the maximum total driving time across any two consecutive weeks?
questions.reference_answer: 90 hours.

questions.question: What is the minimum duration of a reduced weekly rest, and by when must the reduction be compensated?
questions.reference_answer: At least 24 consecutive hours, with the reduction compensated (attached to a rest of at least 9 hours) before the end of the third week following the week in question.

questions.question: In Germany, what is the maximum permitted speed for an e-scooter and the minimum age to ride one?
questions.reference_answer: 20 km/h maximum, minimum age 14, with no driving licence required.

questions.question: In Germany, what is the minimum lateral distance for overtaking a cyclist, and the urban fine for breaching it?
questions.reference_answer: 1.5 m minimum distance; an €80 fine (and one penalty point) in urban areas.

questions.question: Under the French Loi Badinter (1985), what is the only condition under which a road-traffic victim can be denied compensation?
questions.reference_answer: Only where the victim voluntarily (wilfully) caused the accident; otherwise victims are entitled to full compensation regardless of their own fault.
